gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Run `body` within a freshly-opened scope region and close the region after the body returns."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Per-hypha scope-exit entry point (RFC-0027 §10.3)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the structured-concurrency entry point for scope-exit reclamation in one hypha's body:"] # [doc = " \"run the hypha body; reclaim at hypha-exit.\" Each hypha owns exactly one single-threaded"] # [doc = " intra-hypha region (see the cross-hypha FLAG in this module's doc-comment)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # What happens"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " 1. A fresh [`Region`] is opened (`Region::new()`)."] # [doc = " 2. `body(&mut region)` is called. The body may call `region.defer(hash)` for every value"] # [doc = "    whose RC hit zero within this scope (or any scope-local allocation to be freed at exit)."] # [doc = " 3. **After `body` returns** (normal path), `region.close(sink)` is called unconditionally,"] # [doc = "    emitting one `ScopeExit` [`crate::reclamation::ReclamationRecord`] per deferred entry"] # [doc = "    through `sink` and returning the [`ClosedRegion`] summary."] # [doc = " 4. The function returns `(body_result, closed_region)`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Panic behaviour"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " If `body` panics, the region is dropped as the stack unwinds. A panic is an abnormal scope"] # [doc = " abort; in debug builds the underlying [`Region`]'s drop-guard panics again if there are"] # [doc = " deferred entries, surfacing the number of lost audit records (G2 never-silent signal). This"] # [doc = " is intentional — do not catch the panic to silence the guard. The debug double-panic is"] # [doc = " the price of a G2-clean design: silent audit loss is impossible in debug builds."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Nested scopes"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Call `with_region` inside a `body` to nest an inner scope under an outer one. The inner scope"] # [doc = " closes (allocating its [`crate::region::RegionEpoch`]) before the outer body returns, so"] # [doc = " `inner_closed.epoch < outer_closed.epoch` by construction (monotonic counter — `Exact`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tags"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - **`close` always called (normal path):** `Exact` — unconditional call after `body`; no"] # [doc = "   conditional branch can skip it (enforced-by-construction)."] # [doc = " - **Panic path (abnormal abort):** `Exact` — drop fires; debug guard surfaces lost records."] # [doc = " - **Nested epoch ordering:** `Exact` — inherited from `Region::close`'s monotonic counter."] # [doc = " - **Batching perf:** `Declared` — DN-32 §6a; no Mycelium measurement."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Example"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ```rust,ignore"] # [doc = " use mycelium_std_runtime::scope_region::with_region;"] # [doc = " use mycelium_std_runtime::reclamation::CollectingSink;"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " let mut sink = CollectingSink::new();"] # [doc = " let (result, closed) = with_region(&mut sink, |region| {"] # [doc = "     region.defer(some_hash);"] # [doc = "     42"] # [doc = " });"] # [doc = " assert_eq!(result, 42);"] # [doc = " assert_eq!(closed.reclaimed_count, 1);"] # [doc = " ```"] pub fn with_region < R > (sink : & mut dyn ReclamationSink , body : impl FnOnce (& mut Region) -> R ,) -> (R , ClosedRegion) { let mut region = Region :: new () ; let result = body (& mut region) ; let closed = region . close (sink) ; (result , closed) }

# [doc = " An explicit-close scope guard wrapping a [`Region`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " For callers who need to interleave deferrals with other logic between scope entry and exit"] # [doc = " and cannot use the closure form ([`with_region`]). Holds an open [`Region`] and exposes"] # [doc = " `defer`, `deferred_count`, `id`, and a **consuming** `close` that guarantees exactly one"] # [doc = " `Region::close` call."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Never-silent contract (G2)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The caller **MUST** call [`RegionScope::close`] before the guard goes out of scope. There is"] # [doc = " no `Drop` impl that accepts a [`ReclamationSink`] (a sink cannot be threaded through `Drop`"] # [doc = " without violating KC-3 / the never-silent contract); dropping a `RegionScope` that still holds"] # [doc = " deferred entries causes the underlying [`Region`]'s debug-drop-panic to fire, surfacing the"] # [doc = " number of lost audit records. This is the same design discipline as `rc.rs`'s `RcCell` —"] # [doc = " explicit-close is the correct pattern; sink-bearing `Drop` is not."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Example"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ```rust,ignore"] # [doc = " use mycelium_std_runtime::scope_region::RegionScope;"] # [doc = " use mycelium_std_runtime::reclamation::CollectingSink;"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " let mut sink = CollectingSink::new();"] # [doc = " let mut scope = RegionScope::enter();"] # [doc = " scope.defer(hash_a);"] # [doc = " // … interleaved work …"] # [doc = " scope.defer(hash_b);"] # [doc = " let closed = scope.close(&mut sink);"] # [doc = " assert_eq!(closed.reclaimed_count, 2);"] # [doc = " ```"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tags"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - **`close` called exactly once (on `RegionScope::close`):** `Exact` — consuming `close`"] # [doc = "   destructs `self`, so double-close is a compile-time type error."] # [doc = " - **Dropped without `close` (debug builds):** `Exact` — underlying `Region` drop-guard panics"] # [doc = "   if deferred entries remain (G2 never-silent signal)."] # [doc = " - **`defer` / `deferred_count` / `id` accuracy:** `Exact` — delegates to `Region`; all"] # [doc = "   fields are exact functions of the region state."] # [derive (Debug)] pub struct RegionScope { # [doc = " The open region backing this scope guard."] region : Region , }

gaps.snippet: impl RegionScope { # [doc = " Open a new scope guard, allocating a fresh [`Region`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — allocates a unique [`ScopeNodeId`] via the monotonic counter."] # [must_use] pub fn enter () -> Self { RegionScope { region : Region :: new () , } } # [doc = " Defer a value for scope-exit reclamation."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Delegates to [`Region::defer`]. The value's content identity is pushed to the deferred"] # [doc = " list; at [`RegionScope::close`] a `ScopeExit` record is emitted for it."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Calling `defer` after `close` is impossible by type (consuming close destructs `self`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — delegates to `Region::defer`; the entry is pushed exactly once."] pub fn defer (& mut self , value_meta_hash : ContentHash) { self . region . defer (value_meta_hash) ; } # [doc = " Number of values currently deferred in this scope."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — delegates to `Region::deferred_count`."] # [must_use] pub fn deferred_count (& self) -> usize { self . region . deferred_count () } # [doc = " The unique identity of this scope's underlying [`Region`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — delegates to `region.id`; unique monotonic allocation."] # [must_use] pub fn id (& self) -> ScopeNodeId { self . region . id } # [doc = " Close this scope guard, emitting all deferred reclamation records and returning the"] # [doc = " [`ClosedRegion`] summary."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is a **consuming** method — `self` is destructed, making double-close a compile-time"] # [doc = " type error. After `close` returns, the sink has received one `ScopeExit` record per"] # [doc = " deferred entry."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: `Exact` — delegates to [`Region::close`]; one `ScopeExit` record per deferred"] # [doc = " entry, emitted unconditionally (enforced-by-construction)."] pub fn close (mut self , sink : & mut dyn ReclamationSink) -> ClosedRegion { self . region . close (sink) } }
